concepts.name: Nature of Matter
concepts.description: Classification of matter into elements, compounds and mixtures; macroscopic and molecular distinctions between pure substances and mixtures.

concepts.name: States of Matter
concepts.description: Solid, liquid and gas phases; interconversion via heating/cooling; plasma as the fourth state.

concepts.name: Pure Substances and Mixtures
concepts.description: Distinction between elements and compounds; homogeneous and heterogeneous mixtures; separation techniques.

concepts.name: Physical Quantities and SI Units
concepts.description: SI base units, derived units, prefixes; significant figures; accuracy vs precision; scientific notation.

concepts.name: Significant Figures and Error Analysis
concepts.description: Rules for counting and arithmetic with significant figures; absolute and relative error; propagation of uncertainty.

concepts.name: Mole Concept and Avogadro's Number
concepts.description: The mole as SI unit of amount; Avogadro's number; molar mass; interconversion of mass, moles and number of particles.

concepts.name: Stoichiometry
concepts.description: Balancing chemical equations; mole ratios; limiting reagent; theoretical and percent yield calculations.

concepts.name: Concentration Units
concepts.description: Molarity, molality, mole fraction, mass percent, ppm; interconversion of concentration units; dilution calculations.

concepts.name: Atomic Theory
concepts.description: Dalton's atomic theory; discovery of electron (cathode rays), proton (canal rays) and neutron; nuclear model of the atom.

concepts.name: Subatomic Particles
concepts.description: Properties of proton, neutron and electron; atomic number, mass number; isotopes, isobars and isotones.

concepts.name: Electromagnetic Radiation
concepts.description: Wave nature of light; wavelength, frequency, speed; electromagnetic spectrum; relationship E = hν.

concepts.name: Atomic Spectra
concepts.description: Emission and absorption spectra; hydrogen spectrum; Balmer, Lyman and Paschen series; Rydberg equation.

concepts.name: Bohr Model of the Atom
concepts.description: Postulates of Bohr's model; energy levels in hydrogen; calculation of radii, velocities and energies; limitations.

concepts.name: Quantum Numbers
concepts.description: Principal (n), azimuthal (l), magnetic (ml) and spin (ms) quantum numbers; physical significance; allowed values.

concepts.name: Atomic Orbitals
concepts.description: Shapes of s, p, d and f orbitals; nodes (radial and angular); orbital energy diagrams; radial distribution functions.

concepts.name: Electronic Configuration
concepts.description: Aufbau principle, Hund's rule, Pauli exclusion principle; writing configurations; anomalous configurations of Cr and Cu.

concepts.name: Photoelectric Effect and Dual Nature
concepts.description: Einstein's explanation of the photoelectric effect; work function; photon energy; de Broglie wavelength; Heisenberg's uncertainty principle.

concepts.name: Quantum Mechanical Model
concepts.description: Schrödinger wave equation (qualitative); wave function ψ and probability density ψ²; significance of orbital shapes.

concepts.name: Early Classification of Elements
concepts.description: Döbereiner's triads, Newlands' octaves, Mendeleev's periodic table; prediction of undiscovered elements; limitations.

concepts.name: Modern Periodic Law and Table
concepts.description: Modern periodic law based on atomic number; long form of periodic table; periods, groups, blocks (s,p,d,f); position of hydrogen.

concepts.name: Atomic and Ionic Radius
concepts.description: Covalent, van der Waals and ionic radii; trends across periods and down groups; isoelectronic species.

concepts.name: Ionization Energy
concepts.description: First and successive ionization energies; trends in the periodic table; anomalies at Groups 2/13 and 15/16; applications.

concepts.name: Electron Affinity and Electronegativity
concepts.description: Electron gain enthalpy; Pauling and Mulliken electronegativity scales; trends and anomalies; use in predicting bond character.

concepts.name: Periodic Trends Overview
concepts.description: Metallic and non-metallic character; oxidation states; diagonal relationships; anomalous behaviour of lithium and beryllium.

concepts.name: Valency and Oxidation State
concepts.description: Valency from electronic configuration; common oxidation states; variable oxidation states; predicting formulae.

concepts.name: Ionic Bonding
concepts.description: Formation of ions; lattice energy; Born–Haber cycle; factors affecting ionic bond formation; properties of ionic compounds.

concepts.name: Covalent Bonding
concepts.description: Lewis structures; single, double and triple bonds; bond order, length and energy; polar vs non-polar covalent bonds; octet rule and exceptions.

concepts.name: VSEPR Theory
concepts.description: Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion model; prediction of molecular geometry; bond angles; lone pair effects; examples from NH3, H2O, SF6.

concepts.name: Hybridization
concepts.description: sp, sp², sp³, sp³d and sp³d² hybridization; geometry and bond angles; sigma and pi bond framework; examples.

concepts.name: Molecular Orbital Theory
concepts.description: Linear combination of atomic orbitals (LCAO); bonding and antibonding MOs; MO diagrams for H2, O2, N2, NO, CO; bond order and magnetic properties.

concepts.name: Polarity and Dipole Moment
concepts.description: Electronegativity difference and bond polarity; resultant dipole moment; factors affecting molecular dipole; applications.

concepts.name: Intermolecular Forces
concepts.description: van der Waals forces (London dispersion, dipole–dipole, dipole–induced dipole); hydrogen bonding; effect on physical properties.

concepts.name: Metallic Bonding
concepts.description: Electron-sea model; electron delocalization; band theory (qualitative); relation to electrical conductivity, malleability and lustre.

concepts.name: Resonance Structures
concepts.description: Resonance concept; resonance structures and hybrid; stability criteria; examples: ozone, benzene, SO3, CO32−; formal charge.

concepts.name: Bond Parameters
concepts.description: Bond length, bond enthalpy, bond angle and bond order; trends with multiplicity and electronegativity.

concepts.name: Coordinate and Dative Bonding
concepts.description: Formation of coordinate/dative bonds; Lewis acid-base concept; examples: BF3·NH3, H3O+, NH4+; importance in coordination chemistry.

concepts.name: Kinetic Molecular Theory of Gases
concepts.description: Postulates of KMT; pressure as momentum transfer; average kinetic energy proportional to temperature; derivation of ideal gas law qualitatively.

concepts.name: Gas Laws
concepts.description: Boyle's, Charles's and Gay-Lussac's laws; Avogadro's law; ideal gas equation PV = nRT; combined gas law; standard conditions.

concepts.name: Molar Mass from Gas Data
concepts.description: Determining molar mass via gas density; molecular formula from empirical formula and molar mass; Graham's law of effusion.

concepts.name: Real Gases and van der Waals Equation
concepts.description: Deviations from ideal behaviour; compressibility factor Z; van der Waals constants a and b; liquefaction of gases; critical constants.

concepts.name: Liquid State Properties
concepts.description: Vapour pressure; surface tension; viscosity; effect of temperature; capillary action; evaporation and boiling.

concepts.name: Phase Diagrams
concepts.description: Phase equilibria; triple point and critical point; Clausius–Clapeyron equation (qualitative); phase rule (qualitative).

concepts.name: Types of Solutions
concepts.description: Gas-in-gas, gas-in-liquid, liquid-in-liquid, solid-in-liquid solutions; ideal vs non-ideal solutions; like-dissolves-like principle.

concepts.name: Solubility and Henry's Law
concepts.description: Factors affecting solubility; Henry's law for gas solubility; Kh values; applications in carbonation and diving physiology.

concepts.name: Vapour Pressure of Solutions
concepts.description: Raoult's law for volatile and non-volatile solutes; positive and negative deviations; azeotropes; relative lowering of vapour pressure.

concepts.name: Colligative Properties
concepts.description: Elevation of boiling point, depression of freezing point, osmotic pressure; van't Hoff factor for electrolytes; molar mass determination.

concepts.name: Osmosis and Osmotic Pressure
concepts.description: Osmosis mechanism; osmotic pressure calculation; isotonic, hypotonic and hypertonic solutions; reverse osmosis; applications.

concepts.name: Activity and Non-ideal Solutions
concepts.description: Activity coefficient; Debye–Hückel limiting law; ionic strength; activity of electrolytes; deviations in real solutions.

concepts.name: System, Surroundings and State Functions
concepts.description: System vs surroundings; open, closed and isolated systems; state functions vs path functions; extensive and intensive properties.

concepts.name: First Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.description: Internal energy; ΔU = q + w; work done by/on gas (PV work); heat at constant volume (qv) and constant pressure (qp); relationship ΔH = ΔU + ΔnRT.

concepts.name: Enthalpy and Hess's Law
concepts.description: Enthalpy as state function; standard enthalpy of formation, combustion, neutralization; Hess's law; thermochemical equations.

concepts.name: Bond Enthalpy and Born–Haber Cycle
concepts.description: Mean bond enthalpy; estimating ΔH from bond enthalpies; Born–Haber cycle for ionic compounds; lattice enthalpy values.

concepts.name: Entropy and Second Law
concepts.description: Entropy as measure of disorder; second law of thermodynamics; spontaneous processes; standard entropy; entropy changes in reactions.

concepts.name: Gibbs Free Energy and Spontaneity
concepts.description: ΔG = ΔH − TΔS; spontaneity criteria; standard Gibbs energy; relationship ΔG° = −RT ln K; coupling reactions.

concepts.name: Third Law and Absolute Entropy
concepts.description: Third law of thermodynamics; absolute entropy from 0 K; standard molar entropy; residual entropy; application to chemical calculations.

concepts.name: Heat Capacities of Gases
concepts.description: Cp and Cv for ideal gases; γ = Cp/Cv; equipartition theorem (qualitative); Kirchhoff's equation for temperature dependence of ΔH.

concepts.name: Thermodynamics of Cells
concepts.description: Relationship ΔG = −nFE; cell EMF and equilibrium constant; temperature dependence of EMF (qualitative); thermodynamic efficiency.

concepts.name: Equilibrium Concept
concepts.description: Dynamic equilibrium; equilibrium in physical and chemical processes; observable characteristics; importance in industrial chemistry.

concepts.name: Equilibrium Constants Kc and Kp
concepts.description: Expression for Kc and Kp; Kp/Kc relationship; Kc for homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria; calculations with ICE tables.

concepts.name: Le Chatelier's Principle
concepts.description: Effect of concentration, pressure, temperature and catalyst on equilibrium position; van't Hoff isochore; industrial applications (Haber, Contact).

concepts.name: Water Ionization and pH
concepts.description: Ionization of water; Kw; pH scale; strong acids and bases; pOH; calculation of [H+] and pH; neutral, acidic and basic solutions.

concepts.name: Acid–Base Theories
concepts.description: Arrhenius, Brønsted–Lowry and Lewis theories; conjugate acid–base pairs; amphoteric species; Ka, Kb and pKa; relative strength.

concepts.name: Weak Acid and Base Equilibria
concepts.description: Degree of dissociation; Ostwald's dilution law; pH of weak acid/base solutions; simplifying approximations; polyprotic acids.

concepts.name: Buffer Solutions
concepts.description: Henderson–Hasselbalch equation; buffer capacity and range; preparation of buffers; physiological importance; blood buffering.

concepts.name: Salt Hydrolysis
concepts.description: Hydrolysis of salts of weak acid/strong base, strong acid/weak base, and both weak; degree of hydrolysis; pH of salt solutions.

concepts.name: Solubility Equilibria
concepts.description: Solubility product Ksp; relationship between solubility and Ksp; common ion effect; ionic product Q vs Ksp; conditions for precipitation.

concepts.name: Complex Ion Equilibria
concepts.description: Formation constants Kf; stepwise formation; stability of complex ions; competition between precipitation and complex formation.

concepts.name: Acid–Base Titrations
concepts.description: Titration curves for strong–strong, strong–weak and weak–weak combinations; equivalence point pH; choice of indicator.

concepts.name: Oxidation State
concepts.description: Rules for assigning oxidation states; determining change in oxidation state; identifying oxidizing and reducing agents.

concepts.name: Balancing Redox Equations
concepts.description: Oxidation number method; ion-electron (half-reaction) method; balancing in acidic and basic media; net ionic equations.

concepts.name: Disproportionation and Comproportionation
concepts.description: Self-redox reactions; disproportionation of H2O2, Cl2 in NaOH; comproportionation; thermodynamic criteria via reduction potentials.

concepts.name: Types of Redox Reactions
concepts.description: Combination, decomposition, displacement and disproportionation as redox reactions; relation to activity series.

concepts.name: Activity (Electrochemical) Series
concepts.description: Standard reduction potentials; relative reducing power; predicting feasibility of displacement reactions; hydrogen electrode as reference.

concepts.name: Redox Titrations
concepts.description: Permanganometry, dichromate and iodometric titrations; calculation of equivalent and molar mass from titration data.

concepts.name: Galvanic Cell
concepts.description: Cell construction; anode, cathode, salt bridge; cell notation; EMF measurement; relationship ΔG = −nFE; Daniel cell.

concepts.name: Standard Electrode Potential
concepts.description: Standard hydrogen electrode (SHE); measurement of standard reduction potentials; electrochemical series; predicting spontaneity of redox reactions.

concepts.name: Nernst Equation
concepts.description: Concentration dependence of cell EMF; Nernst equation at 298 K; calculation of EMF under non-standard conditions; relation to equilibrium constant.

concepts.name: Concentration Cells
concepts.description: Cells with identical electrodes at different concentrations; EMF of concentration cells; transference and diffusion; applications.

concepts.name: Electrolytic Conductance
concepts.description: Resistance, conductance, conductivity, molar conductivity; Kohlrausch's law; variation with concentration; degree of dissociation from Λm.

concepts.name: Electrolysis and Faraday's Laws
concepts.description: Electrolytic cell; discharge of ions; Faraday's first and second laws; calculation of amount deposited; preferential discharge; industrial applications.

concepts.name: Batteries and Fuel Cells
concepts.description: Primary (Leclanché, alkaline) and secondary (lead-acid, lithium-ion) batteries; hydrogen fuel cell; efficiency and environmental impact.

concepts.name: Corrosion
concepts.description: Electrochemical theory of corrosion; rusting mechanism; factors affecting corrosion; prevention methods (galvanizing, cathodic protection, coating).

concepts.name: Electrosynthesis and Industrial Electrolysis
concepts.description: Chlor-alkali process; electroplating; refining of copper; Hall–Héroult process for aluminium; advantages over chemical reduction.

concepts.name: Rate of Reaction
concepts.description: Average and instantaneous rate; rate in terms of concentration change; factors affecting rate; expressing rate for different stoichiometries.

concepts.name: Rate Law and Order
concepts.description: Rate law expression; order with respect to reactants; overall order; rate constant k and its units; determination by initial-rate method.

concepts.name: Integrated Rate Laws
concepts.description: First-order and second-order integrated rate equations; half-life; graphical determination of order; pseudo-first-order reactions.

concepts.name: Temperature Dependence and Arrhenius Equation
concepts.description: Arrhenius equation; activation energy Ea; frequency factor; Ea from ln k vs 1/T plot; collision theory and transition state theory (qualitative).

concepts.name: Reaction Mechanisms
concepts.description: Elementary steps; molecularity; rate-determining step; steady-state approximation; relationship between mechanism and rate law.

concepts.name: Catalysis
concepts.description: Homogeneous and heterogeneous catalysis; enzyme catalysis (Michaelis–Menten qualitative); catalytic cycle; effect on activation energy; industrial examples.

concepts.name: Photochemical Reactions
concepts.description: Primary and secondary processes; quantum yield; photosensitization; Beer–Lambert law; photochemical ozone formation and destruction.

concepts.name: Crystal Systems
concepts.description: Seven crystal systems; Bravais lattices; unit cells; relationship between crystal system and symmetry elements; X-ray diffraction (Bragg's law qualitative).

concepts.name: Close Packing and Packing Efficiency
concepts.description: Hexagonal close packing (hcp) and cubic close packing (ccp/fcc); body-centred cubic; coordination number; packing efficiency calculation; voids.

concepts.name: Ionic Crystal Structures
concepts.description: Structure of NaCl, CsCl, ZnS, fluorite; radius ratio rule; coordination number; relation to lattice energy.

concepts.name: Crystal Defects
concepts.description: Stoichiometric defects (Schottky, Frenkel); non-stoichiometric defects; impurity defects; effect on density and electrical properties.

concepts.name: Electrical and Magnetic Properties of Solids
concepts.description: Band theory of conductors, semiconductors and insulators; n-type and p-type semiconductors; ferromagnetic, paramagnetic and diamagnetic materials.

concepts.name: Amorphous Solids
concepts.description: Distinction between crystalline and amorphous solids; glass as supercooled liquid; anisotropy; applications of amorphous materials.

concepts.name: Adsorption
concepts.description: Physisorption vs chemisorption; Freundlich and Langmuir adsorption isotherms; factors affecting adsorption; adsorption in heterogeneous catalysis.

concepts.name: Colloids
concepts.description: Colloidal state (1–1000 nm); lyophilic and lyophobic colloids; Tyndall effect; Brownian motion; coagulation; peptization; dialysis.

concepts.name: Emulsions and Gels
concepts.description: Oil-in-water and water-in-oil emulsions; emulsifying agents; gels; foam; practical applications in food, pharmaceuticals and cosmetics.

concepts.name: Surface Tension in Chemistry
concepts.description: Gibbs adsorption equation (qualitative); surfactants; micelle formation; critical micelle concentration; applications in detergency.

concepts.name: Mechanism of Heterogeneous Catalysis
concepts.description: Steps: adsorption, surface reaction, desorption; active sites; poisoning and promotion; industrial examples (Haber, Contact, cracking).

concepts.name: Werner's Theory
concepts.description: Primary and secondary valency; coordination number; complex ion formulation; Werner's experimental evidence; application to metal ammine complexes.

concepts.name: Nomenclature of Complexes
concepts.description: IUPAC rules for naming coordination compounds; naming ligands, central metal and counter ions; anionic and cationic complexes; bridging ligands.

concepts.name: Isomerism in Complexes
concepts.description: Structural isomerism (ionization, hydrate, linkage, coordination); stereoisomerism (geometric cis/trans, optical); conditions for each type.

concepts.name: Crystal Field Theory
concepts.description: Crystal field splitting in octahedral and tetrahedral fields; Δo and Δt; strong-field vs weak-field ligands; spectrochemical series; high-spin and low-spin complexes; colour and magnetism.

concepts.name: Bonding in Coordination Compounds
concepts.description: Valence bond theory of complexes; hybridization in octahedral, square planar and tetrahedral; limitations of VBT; introduction to LFT/MO for complexes.

concepts.name: Stability Constants
concepts.description: Formation constants; EDTA complexation; chelate effect and its thermodynamic origin; applications in analytical chemistry and medicine.

concepts.name: Applications of Coordination Chemistry
concepts.description: Haemoglobin and chlorophyll as coordination compounds; cisplatin in cancer treatment; photography (thiosulfate); extraction metallurgy.

concepts.name: Hydrogen
concepts.description: Position of hydrogen; isotopes; preparation of dihydrogen; properties; hydrides (ionic, covalent, interstitial); water and hydrogen peroxide; fuel cell potential.

concepts.name: Alkali Metals
concepts.description: Electronic configuration and general properties; trends in physical and chemical properties; reactions with O2, water and halogens; oxides and hydroxides; important compounds of Na and K.

concepts.name: Alkaline Earth Metals
concepts.description: General properties; diagonal relationship of Be with Al; anomalous behaviour of Be and Mg; important compounds (CaO, Ca(OH)2, CaCO3, Plaster of Paris, MgSO4).

concepts.name: Water and its Chemistry
concepts.description: Structure; anomalous properties due to H-bonding; hard and soft water; treatment; heavy water D2O; amphoteric nature; water as solvent.

concepts.name: Group 13 — Boron Family
concepts.description: Electronic configuration; diagonal relationship B–Si; properties and compounds of B (borax, boron hydrides); Al production (Hall–Héroult); alums.

concepts.name: Group 14 — Carbon Family
concepts.description: Allotropes of carbon (diamond, graphite, fullerenes); CO and CO2; silicon and silicates; silicones; Ge, Sn, Pb — trends in metallic character.

concepts.name: Group 15 — Nitrogen Family
concepts.description: Nitrogen: diatomic molecule, fixation, ammonia (Haber process), nitric acid (Ostwald process), oxides; phosphorus allotropes, phosphine, oxoacids.

concepts.name: Group 16 — Oxygen Family
concepts.description: Oxygen: allotropic forms, oxides; ozone (preparation, structure, properties); sulphur allotropes; H2S; SO2; sulphuric acid (Contact process) — industrial significance.

concepts.name: Group 17 — Halogens
concepts.description: General trends; Cl2 preparation, properties and uses; HX acids (bond energy, acid strength); halogen oxoacids; interhalogen compounds; fluorine anomaly.

concepts.name: Group 18 — Noble Gases
concepts.description: Electronic configuration and inertness; discovery; preparation (fractional distillation of air); uses; xenon fluorides and oxides (structure and properties).

concepts.name: Trends Across p-Block
concepts.description: Comparison of properties across periods and groups of p-block; inert-pair effect; metallic character; anomalous behaviour of first member; diagonal relationships.

concepts.name: Transition Metals — General Properties
concepts.description: Electronic configurations of d-block elements; variable oxidation states; magnetic properties; coloured ions; catalytic activity; alloy formation; comparison with s and p-block.

concepts.name: First-Row Transition Metals
concepts.description: Individual chemistry of Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn; important compounds and reactions; oxidation state stability; colour and magnetism.

concepts.name: Oxides and Oxyanions of Transition Metals
concepts.description: Chromates and dichromates (Cr2O7²⁻ ⇌ CrO4²⁻ equilibrium); permanganate (MnO4⁻) as oxidising agent; vanadium pentoxide; applications in industrial processes.

concepts.name: Lanthanides and Actinides
concepts.description: Electronic configurations of 4f and 5f elements; lanthanide contraction and its consequences; oxidation states of lanthanides (+3 stable) and actinides; radioactivity of actinides; uses.

concepts.name: Organometallic Chemistry
concepts.description: 18-electron rule; classification of ligands by electron count; Zeise's salt; ferrocene; metal carbonyls; catalysis by organometallics (Wilkinson's, Ziegler–Natta).

concepts.name: IUPAC Nomenclature
concepts.description: Naming of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, cyclic, and branched chains; common functional groups; IUPAC rules for complex molecules; trivial vs systematic names.

concepts.name: Carbon Hybridization
concepts.description: sp3, sp2, sp hybridization in organic molecules; geometry and bond angles; sigma and pi framework; implications for reactivity.

concepts.name: Structural and Stereoisomerism
concepts.description: Chain, position, functional group and metamerism; cis-trans (geometric) isomerism; optical isomerism (chirality, enantiomers, diastereomers); R/S nomenclature; Fischer projections.

concepts.name: Inductive and Mesomeric Effects
concepts.description: Inductive (+I, −I) effects; electron donation and withdrawal; mesomeric (resonance) effects; hyperconjugation; application to stability and reactivity prediction.

concepts.name: Reactive Intermediates
concepts.description: Carbocations (stability order), carbanions (stability), free radicals (stability); generation and reactions; carbenes and nitrenes (introduction).

concepts.name: Organic Reaction Mechanisms
concepts.description: Homolytic and heterolytic fission; nucleophile and electrophile; types: SN1, SN2, E1, E2, electrophilic addition, electrophilic aromatic substitution; drawing mechanisms with arrows.

concepts.name: Purification Techniques
concepts.description: Simple and fractional distillation; sublimation; crystallization; chromatography (TLC, column, HPLC); extraction; melting/boiling point as purity test.

concepts.name: Qualitative Organic Analysis
concepts.description: Detection of elements (Lassaigne's test); functional group tests (Tollens', Fehling's, iodoform, Victor Meyer); IR and mass spec (introduction).

concepts.name: Introduction to Spectroscopy
concepts.description: IR spectroscopy for functional group identification; 1H NMR chemical shift and coupling; mass spectrometry fragmentation patterns; UV-Vis in conjugated systems.

concepts.name: Aromaticity
concepts.description: Hückel's rule (4n+2 π electrons); aromatic, anti-aromatic, non-aromatic systems; benzene structure; stability; heterocyclic aromatic compounds.

concepts.name: Electron Flow and Curved Arrows
concepts.description: Conventions for drawing curved arrows; representing electron flow in bond breaking and making; practice with SN2, E2, and addition mechanisms.

concepts.name: Pericyclic Reactions
concepts.description: Cycloadditions (Diels–Alder [4+2]); electrocyclic reactions; sigmatropic rearrangements; Woodward–Hoffmann rules (qualitative); thermal vs photochemical selectivity.

concepts.name: Alkanes
concepts.description: Sources (petroleum, natural gas); preparation (Wurtz, Kolbe, hydrogenation); physical properties; free-radical halogenation mechanism; combustion; cracking.

concepts.name: Alkenes
concepts.description: Preparation (elimination, dehydrogenation); electrophilic addition (Markovnikov's rule, HBr, H2O, Br2, H2SO4); mechanism; ozonolysis; polymerization.

concepts.name: Alkynes
concepts.description: Preparation from geminal/vicinal dihalides and dehydrohalogenation; acidic nature; electrophilic addition; hydration to ketone (Markovnikov); nucleophilic addition; linear geometry.

concepts.name: Benzene and Arenes
concepts.description: Kekulé structure, resonance hybrid; electrophilic aromatic substitution mechanism (nitration, sulfonation, halogenation, Friedel–Crafts); directive effects of substituents; addition vs substitution.

concepts.name: Conformational Analysis
concepts.description: Newman projections; eclipsed, staggered, gauche conformations of ethane; ring strain in cycloalkanes; chair and boat cyclohexane; axial and equatorial substituents.

concepts.name: Petroleum Refining
concepts.description: Fractional distillation of crude oil; fractions and uses; cracking and reforming; octane number; antiknocking agents; alternatives to fossil fuels.

concepts.name: Polycyclic and Heterocyclic Systems
concepts.description: Naphthalene, anthracene (qualitative); furan, thiophene, pyrrole, pyridine; aromaticity; reactions compared to benzene.

concepts.name: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes — Introduction
concepts.description: Classification; nomenclature; physical properties; C-X bond polarity; relative reactivity of C-F, C-Cl, C-Br, C-I.

concepts.name: SN1 Mechanism
concepts.description: First-order kinetics; carbocation intermediate; racemization; effect of substrate structure; solvent effects; competition with E1.

concepts.name: SN2 Mechanism
concepts.description: Second-order kinetics; backside attack; Walden inversion; steric effects on rate; primary vs tertiary substrates; Finkelstein reaction.

concepts.name: Elimination Reactions
concepts.description: E1 and E2 mechanisms; Saytzeff's rule; Hofmann rule (bulky base); competition between substitution and elimination; factors determining outcome.

concepts.name: Haloarenes
concepts.description: Preparation; resonance deactivation; nucleophilic aromatic substitution (Meisenheimer complex); SNAr vs aryne mechanism; Wurtz–Fittig reaction.

concepts.name: Organolithium and Grignard Reagents
concepts.description: Preparation of RLi and RMgX; reactions with electrophiles (aldehydes, ketones, CO2, esters); quenching; limitations (protic solvents, active hydrogen).

concepts.name: Polyhalogen Compounds and CFCs
concepts.description: CHCl3, CCl4, DDT; CFCs (freons) and ozone depletion; alternatives; environmental regulations.

concepts.name: Alcohols
concepts.description: Classification (1°, 2°, 3°); preparation from haloalkanes, aldehydes/ketones, carboxylic acids; physical properties; hydrogen bonding; reactions: with Na, HX, oxidation, dehydration.

concepts.name: Phenols
concepts.description: Preparation (cumene, benzene sulfonation, diazonium salts); acidic character (pKa ~10, stronger than alcohols); resonance explanation; reactions: electrophilic substitution, esterification.

concepts.name: Ethers
concepts.description: Williamson synthesis; physical properties; reactions with HX (cleavage mechanism); ring opening of epoxides; diethyl ether as solvent.

concepts.name: Diols and Polyols
concepts.description: Preparation of ethylene glycol; reactions (with periodic acid, pinacol rearrangement); glycerol; sugar alcohols; applications in industry.

concepts.name: Epoxides
concepts.description: Preparation from alkenes (mCPBA, halohydrin); acid and base catalysed ring opening (regioselectivity); Payne rearrangement; synthetic utility.

concepts.name: Protecting Group Strategy
concepts.description: Need for protection/deprotection; THP, MOM, TBS ethers; benzyl and acetyl protection; selective reactions; orthogonal protecting groups.

concepts.name: Aldehydes
concepts.description: Preparation (PCC/Swern oxidation, ozonolysis, Rosenmund); physical properties; nucleophilic addition mechanism; reactions with HCN, RMgX, H2NOH, RNHNH2, bisulphite; Cannizzaro reaction.

concepts.name: Ketones
concepts.description: Preparation (Friedel–Crafts, Wacker, hydration of alkynes); comparison with aldehydes in reactivity; Baeyer–Villiger; enol formation and keto-enol tautomerism.

concepts.name: Alpha-Carbon Reactions
concepts.description: Enol and enolate; aldol condensation (acid and base catalysed); crossed aldol; retro-aldol; Claisen condensation; malonic ester synthesis.

concepts.name: Carboxylic Acids
concepts.description: Preparation; acidity and pKa; resonance stabilization of carboxylate; inductive effects on acidity; reactions: esterification, acyl chloride, anhydride, decarboxylation.

concepts.name: Carboxylic Acid Derivatives
concepts.description: Acyl chlorides, anhydrides, esters, amides; reactivity order; nucleophilic acyl substitution mechanism; interconversion; saponification; transesterification.

concepts.name: Named Carbonyl Reactions
concepts.description: Perkin, Knoevenagel, Reformatsky, Wittig, Baeyer–Villiger; mechanism and synthetic significance; planning retrosynthetic sequences.

concepts.name: Spectroscopic Identification of Carbonyls
concepts.description: Characteristic IR absorption of C=O; 1H and 13C NMR of aldehydes and ketones; mass spec α-cleavage and McLafferty rearrangement.

concepts.name: Amines
concepts.description: Classification (1°, 2°, 3°); basicity order (3° > 2° > 1° > NH3 vs aniline); preparation from nitro reduction, alkylation, Gabriel synthesis; reactions: with HNO2, acylation, coupling.

concepts.name: Diazonium Salts
concepts.description: Preparation from aniline; stability; Balz–Schiemann, Sandmeyer reactions; azo coupling; replacement reactions; importance in dye synthesis.

concepts.name: Nitro Compounds
concepts.description: Preparation (nitration of arenes); reduction to aniline (Sn/HCl, catalytic hydrogenation); partial reduction; effect on ring reactivity.

concepts.name: Nitrogen Heterocycles
concepts.description: Pyridine, quinoline, isoquinoline (basicity, electrophilic and nucleophilic substitution); pyrrole, indole, imidazole; biological relevance (DNA bases, alkaloids).

concepts.name: Amino Acids
concepts.description: Structure of α-amino acids; essential vs non-essential; zwitterion; isoelectric point; classification by R group; peptide bond; primary structure of proteins.

concepts.name: Carbohydrates
concepts.description: Classification (mono/di/polysaccharides); glucose structure (Haworth, chair); anomers; mutarotation; disaccharides (sucrose, lactose, maltose); starch, cellulose, glycogen.

concepts.name: Proteins
concepts.description: Primary, secondary (α-helix, β-sheet), tertiary, quaternary structure; disulfide bridges; denaturation; enzymes (substrate specificity, active site); collagen, haemoglobin.

concepts.name: Nucleic Acids
concepts.description: Nucleotide components (base, sugar, phosphate); DNA double helix; hydrogen bonding between base pairs; RNA types (mRNA, tRNA, rRNA); replication and transcription (qualitative).

concepts.name: Lipids
concepts.description: Fatty acids (saturated and unsaturated); triglycerides; saponification; phospholipids; biological membranes; fat-soluble vitamins; essential fatty acids.

concepts.name: Vitamins and Hormones
concepts.description: Water-soluble (B-complex, C) and fat-soluble (A, D, E, K) vitamins; deficiency diseases; enzyme cofactors; steroid and peptide hormones (qualitative).

concepts.name: Enzyme Kinetics
concepts.description: Michaelis–Menten equation; Km and Vmax; turnover number; competitive and non-competitive inhibition; allosteric regulation; lock-and-key vs induced-fit model.

concepts.name: Addition Polymerization
concepts.description: Free-radical, cationic and anionic addition polymerization; chain growth mechanism; polymers: polyethylene, PVC, polystyrene, PTFE, natural rubber.

concepts.name: Condensation Polymerization
concepts.description: Step-growth mechanism; loss of small molecule; polymers: nylon-6, nylon-6,6, Dacron (PET), Bakelite, melamine-formaldehyde; co-polymerization.

concepts.name: Natural Polymers
concepts.description: Starch, cellulose, natural rubber (cis-polyisoprene), silk (fibroin), wool (keratin); latex; vulcanization; comparison with synthetic polymers.

concepts.name: Polymer Properties
concepts.description: Number and weight average molar mass; polydispersity; glass transition temperature; crystallinity; mechanical properties (elastomers, plastics, fibres); biodegradability.

concepts.name: Biodegradable and Functional Polymers
concepts.description: PLA, PHB, PLGA; green chemistry criteria; conducting polymers (polyacetylene, qualitative); liquid crystals; block copolymers; nano-composites.

concepts.name: Atmosphere and Composition
concepts.description: Layers of the atmosphere; composition; residence times of gases; troposphere chemistry; role of CO2, N2, O2; natural greenhouse effect.

concepts.name: Air Pollution
concepts.description: Primary and secondary pollutants; photochemical smog; NOx and hydrocarbons; CO toxicity; particulate matter; acid rain chemistry (SO2/NOx); control strategies.

concepts.name: Ozone Depletion
concepts.description: Chapman cycle; catalytic destruction by Cl from CFCs; polar vortex; Montreal Protocol; recovery timeline; UV-B effects on biology.

concepts.name: Water and Soil Pollution
concepts.description: Eutrophication; BOD and COD; heavy metal contamination; pesticides and their fate; soil acidity; green chemistry principles (12 principles, qualitative).

concepts.name: Gravimetric Analysis
concepts.description: Principles; precipitation, filtration, ignition; calculation from gravimetric factor; sources of error; applications (BaSO4, AgCl); separation and purity.

concepts.name: Volumetric Analysis
concepts.description: Primary standards; standardisation; back-titration; equivalence point vs end point; calculation of unknown concentration; complexometric titrations (EDTA).

concepts.name: Chromatography
concepts.description: Stationary and mobile phases; partition and adsorption chromatography; Rf value (TLC); HPLC; GC; ion-exchange; size-exclusion; applications in purification and analysis.

concepts.name: Spectroscopic Methods
concepts.description: AAS and flame photometry for metals; ICP-OES; UV-Vis for concentration (Beer–Lambert); IR for structural ID; NMR for molecular structure; mass spec for exact mass.
